Title: Efficient multiple gene knockout in Colletotrichum higginsianum via CRISPR/Cas9 ribonucleoprotein and URA3-based marker recycling

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR/Cas9 RNP increased URA3 knockout rate in C. higginsianum to 98%. 2. ura3 mutants showed uridine auxotrophy and partial 5-FOA resistance, confirming loss of URA3-associated enzymatic activity. 3. URA3 knock-in restored colony growth and pathogenicity, and TEF-URA3 served as a recyclable selection marker.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Uridine nucleotide biosynthesis required for fungal growth; URA3 also converts 5-FOA into toxic compounds used for counterselection.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ura3 mutation caused uridine auxotrophy and loss of pathogenicity, requiring URA3 knock-in before virulence assessment.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; knock-in complementation; PCR screening; colony growth assay with/without uridine; 5-FOA sensitivity assay; plant inoculation assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of URA3 caused uridine auxotrophy, partial 5-FOA resistance, and loss of pathogenicity; URA3 knock-in restored these phenotypes.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used primarily as a recyclable selection marker in the study rather than as a target for nutrient improvement.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Dehydrates scytalone in the DHN-melanin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key pathway step supported; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for melanization of appressoria and pathogenicity/virulence on Arabidopsis thaliana.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for synthesis of a direct melanin precursor and thus for terminal product formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SCD1 knockout caused melanin-deficient transparent appressoria, scytalone accumulation, and loss of pathogenicity.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SCD1 was efficiently knocked out using CRISPR/Cas9 and TEF-URA3 selection. 2. scd1 mutants formed transparent, melanin-deficient appressoria and lost pathogenicity. 3. UPLC-MS detected scytalone accumulation only in scd1 mutants, confirming SCD1 acts downstream of scytalone in melanin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; marker recycling; PCR genotyping; microscopy of appressoria; UPLC-MS metabolite profiling; plant infection assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro appressorium induction; plant inoculation on Arabidopsis thaliana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of melanin caused loss of pathogenicity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for antifungal control or virulence disruption through melanin pathway inhibition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Scytalone accumulation in scd1 but not pks1 scd1 strongly supports enzyme placement downstream of PKS1.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: A precursor leading to scytalone production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes an upstream polyketide precursor required for scytalone production in the DHN-melanin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream pathway step supported; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for melanization of appressoria and pathogenicity/virulence on Arabidopsis thaliana.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for downstream melanin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PKS1 knockout in the scd1 background prevented scytalone accumulation and contributed to melanin-deficient, nonpathogenic phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PKS1 was sequentially knocked out after SCD1 using the reusable TEF-URA3 marker. 2. pks1 scd1 double mutants remained melanin deficient and nonpathogenic. 3. Scytalone was absent in pks1 scd1 mutants, supporting PKS1 function upstream of scytalone synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Sequential CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; marker recycling; PCR genotyping; microscopy of appressoria; UPLC-MS metabolite profiling; plant infection assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro appressorium induction; plant inoculation on Arabidopsis thaliana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of melanin caused loss of pathogenicity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target for blocking melanin-dependent infection structures.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as ChPKS1/ChPKS19 homologue; function inferred and supported by mutant metabolite phenotype relative to scd1.
